note: Adding with_counts param to groupby filter (do_groupby function in filters.py). This is my only file to touch.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add case_sensitive param to groupby() filter (sync+async). Only touches filters.py. What file(s) does your feature need? If it also needs filters.py, let's figure out how to split (e.g. by function) or take turns.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Confirmed: we both touch do_groupby/sync_do_groupby/async do_groupby in filters.py, so file-level split won't work - let's take turns instead. Proposal: you go first since you replied first. Implement case_sensitive, run tests, then send DONE with a summary of the exact signature/behavior change you made. I'll then pull current file state, add with_counts on top of your change, and send my own DONE. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Both our features touch do_groupby() in filters.py, so we can't get a disjoint file split. Proposing sequential turns instead: I'll implement case_sensitive first (small, isolated change to the sort key + grouping), send DONE, then you add with_counts on top of my updated file so we don't clobber each other. I'll start now and ping you when done - please hold off editing filters.py until you get my DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed. I'll hold off editing filters.py until I receive your DONE, then layer with_counts on top of your case_sensitive change.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed - I'll go first. Implementing case_sensitive param now, will run tests, then send DONE with the exact signature/behavior summary before you touch the file.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby. Signature change: sync_do_groupby(environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False) and async do_groupby same signature. Behavior: when case_sensitive=False (default), sort/group key uses ignore_case postprocessor; the emitted grouper key is the FIRST item's original (non-lowercased) attribute value via a second make_attrgetter call with no postprocess. When case_sensitive=True, behavior is unchanged from before (exact case-sensitive sort+group). Implementation is confined to sync_do_groupby and do_groupby (lines ~1160-1281) - _GroupTuple, make_attrgetter, ignore_case, groupby, auto_to_list all reused unchanged. All existing groupby tests pass (tests/test_filters.py -k groupby, 4 passed) plus full filters test suite (122 passed). File state is ready for you to layer with_counts on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Implemented with_counts param on top of current sync_do_groupby/do_groupby (my copy didn't show your case_sensitive edits directly - looks like we're in separate environments - but the change is additive/isolated: when with_counts=True, yields plain (key, list, count) tuples instead of _GroupTuple; default path unchanged. All 122 filters tests pass. Patch written to patch.txt, touches only filters.py.
